title: LLM Token Usage, Cost Attribution, and Quota Burn-Rate Monitoring (OpenAI, Azure OpenAI, AWS Bedrock, Anthropic, Vertex AI, AI gateways, OTel gen_ai, self-hosted usage)

premiumApps.name: Splunk ITSI
premiumApps.note: optional — episode analytics grouping token burn anomalies by tenant and model entities when KPIs consume gateway metrics

dataSources: index=ai_proxy with sourcetype=openai:chat:completion for OpenAI-compatible usage blocks; index=ai_proxy with sourcetype=azure_openai:billing for subscription rollups; index=aws_bedrock with sourcetype=aws:bedrock:invoke for model invocation token counts; index=ai_gateway with sourcetype=litellm:proxy for standardized team metadata; index=ai_otel with sourcetype=otel:gen_ai:metrics for semantic convention token series; index=cmdb with sourcetype=ai_tenant_inventory for owner_team, business_unit, monthly_budget_usd, daily_token_quota; KV lookup llm_model_pricing.csv for model, in_per_1k_usd, out_per_1k_usd, tier, region
app: Splunk HTTP Event Collector plus Splunk OpenTelemetry Collector patterns + enterprise AI gateway exporters + cloud billing connectors
description: Detects anomalous LLM token consumption, accelerating dollar burn against budgets, daily quota pacing stress, per-tenant and per-project misallocation, model-class cost regressions, prompt-bloat patterns, and attribution gaps by fusing proxy completions, cloud billing rollups, Bedrock invokes, gateway logs, OTel gen_ai metrics, pricing lookups, and tenant inventory joins.
value: FinOps and platform leaders receive an explainable, multi-source ledger that separates benign batch jobs from runaway retrieval inflation, highlights silent upgrades from economy to premium model classes, and routes incidents with owner_team context before invoices and executive reviews become surprises, while engineering keeps quota pressure visible alongside marginal cost per thousand tokens.
grandmaExplanation: We watch one big apartment tower with a shared electric meter and a shared water main. Every flat runs lights, kettles, washers, and hoses, and we meter each room so one renter cannot hide an industrial pump while everyone else pays the surprise bill. When a tap goes wild, we see which flat and which hourly spike caused it before the super has to guess who to visit.
implementation: Land OpenAI-compatible completion logs, Azure OpenAI billing rollups, Amazon Bedrock invoke records, AI gateway proxy lines, and OpenTelemetry gen_ai token metrics into Splunk; publish llm_model_pricing.csv and cmdb ai_tenant_inventory; normalize tenant_id, project, and model; save platform_uc_13_4_2_llm_token_cost_quota_burn every five minutes with earliest=-24h@h; route critical and high severities to platform FinOps and ML gateway on-call; archive weekly evidence exports with chargeback tags.
detailedImplementation:
Step 1 — Prerequisites

Head of Platform owns this control jointly with ML platform engineering, FinOps, identity and access management, application security, data platform stewards, and the observability teams that operate OpenTelemetry Collectors plus HTTP Event Collector landing zones for AI traffic. This use case is deliberately not UC-13.4.1 latency and error fusion, and it is not UC-13.4.3 accelerator duty-cycle economics on GPUs and TPUs. Here the evidence story is token semantics, marginal dollar burn, quota pacing, and attribution: who consumed which model class for which business project, whether spend is climbing faster than budgets and daily token ceilings allow, and whether a routing change quietly upgraded the world from an economy tokenizer to a premium reasoning stack without a matching budget motion.

Token accounting is vendor-shaped. OpenAI-compatible chat completion responses usually publish prompt, completion, and total counters under nested usage objects; your parsers must preserve integers without rounding and must not drop cached-token hints when you later argue about bill reconciliation. Azure OpenAI couples per-deployment quotas and subscription-scoped billing exports; the platform must know deployment names, SKU tiers, and region because list prices and included quota units differ materially from pay-as-you-go public endpoints. Amazon Bedrock emits model invocation records that may arrive via CloudWatch Logs subscription filters, direct service logs, or curated export paths; provisioned throughput and on-demand pricing diverge, and burst behavior interacts with account-level service quotas in ways raw token counters alone will not explain. Anthropic usage patterns mirror message payloads with distinct input and output token fields; combine them with organization and workspace identifiers that match your internal tenant registry. Google Vertex AI billing joins model publishers, locations, and SKU labels; normalize publisher strings before you join llm_model_pricing.csv or you will invent false cost regressions. AI gateway proxies such as LiteLLM, Portkey, or in-house Envoy filters can standardize metadata headers for tenant_id, project, environment, and model_alias; treat the gateway as the system of record for attribution when direct provider logs lack stable application labels.

OpenTelemetry continues to harden generative semantic conventions for gen_ai.client.token.usage style metrics and span attributes that carry operation names, response model ids, and token tallies. When instrumented clients emit OTLP metrics to index=ai_otel with sourcetype=otel:gen_ai:metrics, you gain a vendor-neutral lane that survives a provider swap as long as semantic mapping stays disciplined. Self-hosted OpenAI-compatible servers including vLLM and Ollama can publish token usage in response bodies or side-channel logs; ingest those lines with the same normalization macros you use for public cloud traffic so FinOps does not maintain parallel math.

RBAC is mandatory. Read-only automation accounts that pull billing exports and usage APIs must use workload identities or short-lived tokens stored in Splunk credential storage, not personal administrator keys on laptops. Legal and privacy reviewers must decide whether prompts, completions, or customer identifiers may appear in gateway logs; when redaction is mandatory, configure ingest-time processors and document residual fields used for cost math. Network architects should confirm collectors reach provider endpoints through approved egress without TLS interception that breaks certificate pinning.

Index design lands complementary sourcetypes without forcing one parser to pretend it understands every vendor. Typical patterns include index=ai_proxy for sourcetype=openai:chat:completion structured logs from reverse proxies or application middleware, index=ai_proxy for sourcetype=azure_openai:billing hourly or daily rollups from cost management exports, index=aws_bedrock for sourcetype=aws:bedrock:invoke model invocation narratives, index=ai_gateway for sourcetype=litellm:proxy gateway lines with standardized team metadata, and index=ai_otel for OTLP-derived gen_ai metrics lines. Retention should cover at least thirty days for monthly budget attestations and enough trailing hours to judge burn-rate slopes during mid-month surprises. Stamp change_ticket_id on maintenance that touches routers, prompt templates, or retrieval indexes so validators correlate benign spikes.

Field normalization expectations: tenant_id is a stable billing or security boundary such as subscription short code, AWS account alias, Entra tenant id, or internal FinOps chargeback key. project is a product or initiative slice used for showback, not merely a Kubernetes namespace unless you explicitly map namespaces to budgets. model is the provider-facing model identifier after aliases resolve, not marketing nicknames alone. owner_team and business_unit come from cmdb style inventory rather than free text in logs. monthly_budget_usd and daily_token_quota must be numeric fields maintained by finance partners; bad CMDB rows break severity in ways that look like incidents but are data defects.

Risk briefing: silent prompt bloat from retrieval inflation, chain-of-thought injection, or debugging wrappers that paste entire JSON blobs into prompts can multiply input tokens overnight without a single additional end user. Model class drift from gpt-4o-mini style routes to full gpt-4o or Opus-class models shows up as cost-per-outcome spikes even when request counts look flat. Unauthorized model usage may be a developer experiment, a misconfigured default in a shared library, or an abused API key; combine gateway deny lists with this analytics control rather than pretending analytics replaces policy enforcement. Batch API discounts and reserved capacity change effective marginal price; refresh llm_model_pricing.csv when contracts renew or FinOps publishes new unit economics. Multimodal inputs tokenize images and audio under provider-specific rules; do not compare a text-only baseline to a vision-heavy rollout without adjusting expectations.

Differentiation recap: token usage, dollar attribution, quota burn pacing, and budget guardrails for UC-13.4.2, not UC-13.4.1 HTTP latency and error-rate fusion, not UC-13.4.3 DCGM and kube alloc utilization for accelerators.

FinOps linkage: maintain llm_model_pricing.csv with columns model, in_per_1k_usd, out_per_1k_usd, tier, and region. Version the file when list prices change mid-month. Document currency and tax handling outside Splunk. For chargeback, join business_unit to executive dashboards and keep blended cost per business outcome as a quarterly narrative built from this detection grain plus outcome counters from product analytics stores.

SLO companion: mirror summarized tuples into a summary index keyed by tenant_id, project, and model for multi-week burn reports when executive committees review AI run-rate.

Step 2 — Configure data collection

Stand up five complementary lanes the multisearch fans wide, plus CSV governance artifacts that keep unit prices and tenant budgets out of SPL literals.

Lane A, OpenAI-compatible proxy completion logs: configure reverse proxies or application middleware to emit structured JSON for each chat completion including usage.prompt_tokens, usage.completion_tokens, usage.total_tokens, model, api_version, request_id, and stable tenant headers. Map headers to tenant_id and project. If you must hash api keys, keep a salted mapping table outside Splunk for investigations. Ensure time zones align to UTC at ingest.

Lane B, Azure OpenAI billing and usage rollups: export cost management or marketplace style datasets into index=ai_proxy with sourcetype=azure_openai:billing. Normalize subscription_id to tenant_id, resource_group to project where appropriate, and model_name to the same strings used in runtime logs. Pair exports with Azure Monitor diagnostics for quota and throttling context when latency UC-13.4.1 already lands those lines; do not duplicate full prompts here if policy forbids.

Lane C, Amazon Bedrock model invocations: land CloudWatch or service-level invoke records with model id, account, Region, input and output token counts, and inference profile identifiers. Distinguish on-demand spend from provisioned throughput purchases when enriching recommended_action narratives. Join organization guardrails that block models to separate policy denials from cost anomalies.

Lane D, AI gateway proxies: standardize LiteLLM or Portkey logs to include team_id, metadata_project, requested_model, routed_model, cache_hit flags, and retry counts. Retries can double token totals without additional user value; capture http_status and retry_attempt to explain apparent duplication.

Lane E, OpenTelemetry gen_ai metrics: configure collectors to emit gen_ai.client.token.usage or equivalent metric names with labels for gen_ai.response.model, tenant, and application. Route to index=ai_otel with sourcetype=otel:gen_ai:metrics. Validate exemplars during canary deployments.

Lookup maintenance: publish llm_model_pricing.csv weekly from FinOps spreadsheets with explicit version notes. Publish cmdb sourcetype=ai_tenant_inventory with tenant_id, owner_team, business_unit, monthly_budget_usd, daily_token_quota, and optional outcome_metric_hint for blended KPI reviews. Refresh inventory when reorganizations land.

Example curl helpers from hardened bastions with scoped tokens:

curl -fsS -H "Authorization: Bearer $OPENAI_ADMIN" "https://api.openai.com/v1/organization/usage?date=2026-04-27" | head
curl -fsS -H "Authorization: Bearer $AZURE_TOKEN" "https://management.azure.com/subscriptions/$SUB/providers/Microsoft.CostManagement/query?api-version=2023-03-01" | head
aws bedrock list-foundation-models --region us-east-1 | head

props.conf guidance: set TRUNCATE safely for wide JSON, honor provider timestamps in TIME_PREFIX, and FIELDALIAS alternate token field names to prompt_tokens and completion_tokens when vendors differ. transforms.conf can hash raw prompts while preserving counts.

Pre-save validation: confirm each sourcetype returns rows in the last hour during business traffic, that a synthetic premium model route in lab raises est_line_usd against llm_model_pricing.csv, and that cmdb inventory joins resolve owner_team without null spikes.

Step 3 — Create the search and alert

Save the SPL as platform_uc_13_4_2_llm_token_cost_quota_burn with a five-minute schedule during steady production, dispatch.earliest_time=-24h@h, dispatch.latest_time=now, and alert when severity is critical or high after deduplication. Throttle duplicate tenant_id, project, model tuples for thirty minutes unless severity escalates. Include recommended_action, monthly_budget_burn_pct, and daily_quota_burn_pct in pager bodies so responders open gateway dashboards, FinOps workbooks, and retrieval pipelines without guesswork.

Pipeline narrative: multisearch merges proxy completion logs, Azure billing rollups, Bedrock invoke records, gateway proxy lines, and OTel gen_ai metrics so a silent single lane cannot hide a runaway burn. bucket span=5m aligns series before stats sums prompt, completion, and total tokens and estimated dollar lines from lookup-enriched per-token rates. streamstats window=12 approximates rolling one-hour token and cost sums per tenant, project, and model. streamstats window=288 approximates twenty-four-hour sums for daily quota pacing versus monthly budget ratios. eventstats median(total_tokens) per tenant builds adaptive volume context without quarterly spreadsheet edits. streamstats window=6 on dq_pressure approximates sustained thirty-minute pressure against daily quota fair share derived from hourly burn. case() maps combined signals into severity tiers tied to recommended_action tokens. join enriches llm_model_pricing.csv for marginal rates and cmdb ai_tenant_inventory for owner_team, business_unit, budgets, and quotas. coalesce() ladders absorb field renames across vendors.

Fenced SPL must match the spl JSON field exactly:

```spl
`comment("UC-13.4.2 token usage cost attribution quota burn-rate fusion OpenAI proxy Azure billing Bedrock LiteLLM OTel gen_ai earliest=-24h@h bucket=5m")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=ai_proxy sourcetype=openai:chat:completion earliest=-24h@h latest=now
      | eval tenant_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(tenant_id, organization_id, "unknown_tenant"))))
      | eval project=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(project, app_id, "default_project"))))
      | eval model=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(model, response_model, "unknown_model"))))
      | eval prompt_tokens=tonumber(trim(toString(coalesce(prompt_tokens, usage_prompt_tokens, 0))))
      | eval completion_tokens=tonumber(trim(toString(coalesce(completion_tokens, usage_completion_tokens, 0))))
      | eval total_tokens=tonumber(trim(toString(coalesce(total_tokens, usage_total_tokens, prompt_tokens+completion_tokens))))
      | eval lane="openai_proxy_chat_completion"
      | fields _time tenant_id project model prompt_tokens completion_tokens total_tokens lane ]
    [ search index=ai_proxy sourcetype=azure_openai:billing earliest=-24h@h latest=now
      | eval tenant_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(subscription_id, tenant_id, "unknown_tenant"))))
      | eval project=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(resource_group, workload, "azure_default_project"))))
      | eval model=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(model_name, model_id, "unknown_model"))))
      | eval prompt_tokens=tonumber(trim(toString(coalesce(prompt_tokens, input_tokens, 0))))
      | eval completion_tokens=tonumber(trim(toString(coalesce(completion_tokens, output_tokens, 0))))
      | eval total_tokens=tonumber(trim(toString(coalesce(total_tokens, prompt_tokens+completion_tokens))))
      | eval lane="azure_openai_billing_rollup"
      | fields _time tenant_id project model prompt_tokens completion_tokens total_tokens lane ]
    [ search index=aws_bedrock sourcetype=aws:bedrock:invoke earliest=-24h@h latest=now
      | eval tenant_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(aws_account_id, aws_account, "unknown_tenant"))))
      | eval project=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(bedrock_workload, project_name, "bedrock_default_project"))))
      | eval model=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(model_id, foundation_model, model, "unknown_model"))))
      | eval prompt_tokens=tonumber(trim(toString(coalesce(input_token_count, prompt_token_count, 0))))
      | eval completion_tokens=tonumber(trim(toString(coalesce(output_token_count, completion_token_count, 0))))
      | eval total_tokens=tonumber(trim(toString(coalesce(total_token_count, prompt_tokens+completion_tokens))))
      | eval lane="aws_bedrock_invoke_model"
      | fields _time tenant_id project model prompt_tokens completion_tokens total_tokens lane ]
    [ search index=ai_gateway sourcetype=litellm:proxy earliest=-24h@h latest=now
      | eval tenant_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(team_id, user_api_key_alias, tenant_id, "unknown_tenant"))))
      | eval project=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(metadata_project, project, "litellm_default_project"))))
      | eval model=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(model, requested_model, "unknown_model"))))
      | eval prompt_tokens=tonumber(trim(toString(coalesce(prompt_tokens, proxy_prompt_tokens, 0))))
      | eval completion_tokens=tonumber(trim(toString(coalesce(completion_tokens, proxy_completion_tokens, 0))))
      | eval total_tokens=tonumber(trim(toString(coalesce(total_tokens, prompt_tokens+completion_tokens))))
      | eval lane="litellm_ai_gateway_proxy"
      | fields _time tenant_id project model prompt_tokens completion_tokens total_tokens lane ]
    [ search index=ai_otel sourcetype=otel:gen_ai:metrics earliest=-24h@h latest=now
      | eval tenant_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(tenant_id, organization_id, "unknown_tenant"))))
      | eval project=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(gen_ai_application, project, "otel_genai_project"))))
      | eval model=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(gen_ai_response_model, model, "unknown_model"))))
      | eval prompt_tokens=tonumber(trim(toString(coalesce(gen_ai_usage_input_tokens, 0))))
      | eval completion_tokens=tonumber(trim(toString(coalesce(gen_ai_usage_output_tokens, 0))))
      | eval total_tokens=tonumber(trim(toString(coalesce(gen_ai_usage_total_tokens, prompt_tokens+completion_tokens))))
      | eval lane="otel_gen_ai_semconv_token_metrics"
      | fields _time tenant_id project model prompt_tokens completion_tokens total_tokens lane ]
| eval tenant_id=coalesce(tenant_id,"unknown_tenant")
| eval model=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(model,"unknown_model"))))
| eval model_key=model
| join type=left max=0 model_key
    [| inputlookup llm_model_pricing.csv
     | eval model_key=lower(trim(toString(model)))
     | fields model_key in_per_1k_usd out_per_1k_usd tier region]
| eval in_per_1k_usd=tonumber(coalesce(in_per_1k_usd,0.002))
| eval out_per_1k_usd=tonumber(coalesce(out_per_1k_usd,0.006))
| eval est_line_usd=round((prompt_tokens/1000)*in_per_1k_usd + (completion_tokens/1000)*out_per_1k_usd,6)
| eval tokens_per_request=total_tokens
| eval completion_to_prompt_ratio=if(prompt_tokens>0, round(completion_tokens/prompt_tokens,4), null())
| eval model_class_drift=case(match(model_key,"mini|3.5|haiku|flash"),"economy_class", match(model_key,"turbo|4o-mini|sonnet|pro"),"balanced_class", match(model_key,"4o$|^gpt-4[^-]|opus|o1|thinking"),"premium_class", true(),"unclassified_class")
| bucket _time span=5m
| stats sum(prompt_tokens) AS prompt_tokens sum(completion_tokens) AS completion_tokens sum(total_tokens) AS total_tokens sum(est_line_usd) AS est_cost_usd max(tokens_per_request) AS tokens_per_request max(completion_to_prompt_ratio) AS completion_to_prompt_ratio values(lane) AS lanes first(model_class_drift) AS model_class_drift BY _time tenant_id project model_key
| eval model=model_key
| streamstats window=12 global=f current=t sum(est_cost_usd) AS est_cost_usd_1h sum(total_tokens) AS rolling_tokens_1h BY tenant_id project model
| streamstats window=288 global=f current=t sum(est_cost_usd) AS cost_24h sum(total_tokens) AS rolling_tokens_24h BY tenant_id project model
| eventstats median(total_tokens) AS fleet_token_p50 BY tenant_id
| eventstats avg(est_cost_usd) AS env_avg_cost_bucket
| join type=left max=0 tenant_id
    [| search index=cmdb sourcetype=ai_tenant_inventory earliest=-7d@h latest=now
     | dedup tenant_id
     | eval tenant_id=lower(trim(toString(tenant_id)))
     | fields tenant_id owner_team business_unit monthly_budget_usd daily_token_quota]
| eval monthly_budget_usd=tonumber(coalesce(monthly_budget_usd,250000))
| eval daily_token_quota=tonumber(coalesce(daily_token_quota,500000000))
| eval dq_pressure=if(daily_token_quota>0, round(100*rolling_tokens_1h/(daily_token_quota/24.0),4), 0)
| streamstats window=6 global=f current=t avg(dq_pressure) AS dq_pressure_30m BY tenant_id project model
| eval monthly_budget_burn_pct=if(monthly_budget_usd>0, round(100*cost_24h/monthly_budget_usd,4), 0)
| eval daily_quota_burn_pct=if(daily_token_quota>0, round(100*rolling_tokens_24h/daily_token_quota,4), 0)
| eval severity=case(monthly_budget_burn_pct>90 OR dq_pressure_30m>80, "critical", monthly_budget_burn_pct>85 OR dq_pressure_30m>65 OR daily_quota_burn_pct>85, "high", monthly_budget_burn_pct>70 OR daily_quota_burn_pct>70 OR total_tokens>3*coalesce(fleet_token_p50,1), "medium", total_tokens>1.5*coalesce(fleet_token_p50,1) OR est_cost_usd>2*coalesce(env_avg_cost_bucket,0.000001), "low", true(), "info")
| eval recommended_action=case(severity=="critical" AND monthly_budget_burn_pct>90, "halt_discretionary_rollouts_renegotiate_budget_or_shift_traffic_to_economy_models", severity=="critical" AND dq_pressure_30m>80, "enforce_quota_throttles_partition_keys_and_request_capacity_uplift", severity=="high", "trace_model_router_changes_revert_unapproved_upgrades_validate_rag_context_limits", severity=="medium", "tighten_gateway_metadata_tags_review_prompt_templates_and_batch_policies", severity=="low", "sample_requests_compare_tokenizers_and_multimodal_attachment_rules", true(), "continue_finops_reconciliation_refresh_price_lookup_and_cmdb_budgets")
| table _time tenant_id project owner_team business_unit model total_tokens prompt_tokens completion_tokens est_cost_usd_1h monthly_budget_usd monthly_budget_burn_pct daily_quota_burn_pct severity recommended_action
```

savedsearches.conf sketch:

[platform_uc_13_4_2_llm_token_cost_quota_burn_alert]
cron_schedule = */5 * * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -24h@h
dispatch.latest_time = now
enableSched = 1
action.email = 1
action.email.to = platform-ai-finops-oncall@example.com
action.email.subject = LLM token burn $result.severity$ tenant=$result.tenant_id$ project=$result.project$
counttype = number of events
relation = greater than
quantity = 0
search = | savedsearch platform_uc_13_4_2_llm_token_cost_quota_burn | where severity IN ("critical","high")

Performance: if Job Inspector shows multisearch queue time above objectives, materialize per-arm summaries hourly into summary indexes keyed on tenant_id, project, and model, widen alert windows on summaries, and retain this full search for investigations.

Step 4 — Validate

Ground truth begins in FinOps systems: compare OpenAI organization usage dashboards, Azure cost analysis blades, AWS Cost Explorer with Bedrock filters, Anthropic admin usage pages, and Vertex billing exports against Splunk rows for the same hour. When Splunk lags, verify HEC token health, object storage ingest delays, and collector memory limiter backpressure before muting detections.

Deep dive when monthly_budget_burn_pct rises while daily_quota_burn_pct looks calm: inspect blended price changes, tax lines, or currency normalization rather than assuming token miscounts. Conversely, when daily_quota_burn_pct spikes with modest dollars, you may be on an economy model with enormous prompt tokens from retrieval inflation; open retrieval metrics and chunk size policies.

Quota validation: in a non-production tenant, raise synthetic traffic against a capped daily_token_quota and confirm dq_pressure and severity escalate, then restore limits and verify decay within two windows.

Negative test: after recovery when gateway weights, retrieval indexes, and model defaults return to approved baselines, confirm filtering severity to critical and high returns zero rows for six consecutive runs.

Runbook linkage: document planned migration windows and batch encodings so validators do not file defects during approved maintenance.

Step 5 — Operationalize & Troubleshoot

Case 1 — Developer enabled debug=true logging full prompts into a shared index: redact payloads at ingest, rotate keys, and patch libraries so token counters remain without raw text leakage.

Case 2 — RAG retrieved twelve thousand context tokens after a corpus refresh: shrink chunk sizes, re-tune top-k, and add hierarchical summaries before alerts burn monthly budgets.

Case 3 — A/B test promoted gpt-4o to one hundred percent traffic: verify experiment closure, roll back shadow weights, and reconcile FinOps approval for premium traffic share.

Case 4 — Runaway agentic loop called a tool fifty times per request: add circuit breakers, cap max_iterations, and trace orchestration frameworks for recursion guards.

Case 5 — Bedrock provisioned throughput burst credits depleted: shift traffic to on-demand routes within policy, purchase additional units, or throttle tenants until credits recover.

Case 6 — Quota tier mid-month exhausted for a subscription: request service quota increases, shard workloads across accounts, or enforce per-tenant concurrency limits at the gateway.

Case 7 — Spend exceeds executive budget guardrail even though latency SLOs are green: prioritize FinOps review using this UC rows, not UC-13.4.1 latency panels alone.

Case 8 — Rogue tenant fanned out batch jobs across many API keys: consolidate keys, enforce organization policies, and block abusive metadata patterns at the proxy.

Case 9 — Misconfigured retry policy doubled tokens on transient 429 responses: tune exponential backoff and idempotency keys; dedupe client-side retries in gateway logs.

Case 10 — Embeddings cost regression after switching dimensions: validate embedding model pricing rows, rebuild indexes under change control, and separate embedding dashboards from chat alerts.

Case 11 — Cost spike from prompt-template version bump: diff template releases, restore prior version under incident command, and add governance on template publishing paths.

Case 12 — Missing tenant attribution defaulted all spend to a single project: fix header injection in services, rebuild cmdb keys, and replay attribution corrections with finance partners.
spl:
`comment("UC-13.4.2 token usage cost attribution quota burn-rate fusion OpenAI proxy Azure billing Bedrock LiteLLM OTel gen_ai earliest=-24h@h bucket=5m")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=ai_proxy sourcetype=openai:chat:completion earliest=-24h@h latest=now
      | eval tenant_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(tenant_id, organization_id, "unknown_tenant"))))
      | eval project=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(project, app_id, "default_project"))))
      | eval model=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(model, response_model, "unknown_model"))))
      | eval prompt_tokens=tonumber(trim(toString(coalesce(prompt_tokens, usage_prompt_tokens, 0))))
      | eval completion_tokens=tonumber(trim(toString(coalesce(completion_tokens, usage_completion_tokens, 0))))
      | eval total_tokens=tonumber(trim(toString(coalesce(total_tokens, usage_total_tokens, prompt_tokens+completion_tokens))))
      | eval lane="openai_proxy_chat_completion"
      | fields _time tenant_id project model prompt_tokens completion_tokens total_tokens lane ]
    [ search index=ai_proxy sourcetype=azure_openai:billing earliest=-24h@h latest=now
      | eval tenant_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(subscription_id, tenant_id, "unknown_tenant"))))
      | eval project=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(resource_group, workload, "azure_default_project"))))
      | eval model=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(model_name, model_id, "unknown_model"))))
      | eval prompt_tokens=tonumber(trim(toString(coalesce(prompt_tokens, input_tokens, 0))))
      | eval completion_tokens=tonumber(trim(toString(coalesce(completion_tokens, output_tokens, 0))))
      | eval total_tokens=tonumber(trim(toString(coalesce(total_tokens, prompt_tokens+completion_tokens))))
      | eval lane="azure_openai_billing_rollup"
      | fields _time tenant_id project model prompt_tokens completion_tokens total_tokens lane ]
    [ search index=aws_bedrock sourcetype=aws:bedrock:invoke earliest=-24h@h latest=now
      | eval tenant_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(aws_account_id, aws_account, "unknown_tenant"))))
      | eval project=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(bedrock_workload, project_name, "bedrock_default_project"))))
      | eval model=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(model_id, foundation_model, model, "unknown_model"))))
      | eval prompt_tokens=tonumber(trim(toString(coalesce(input_token_count, prompt_token_count, 0))))
      | eval completion_tokens=tonumber(trim(toString(coalesce(output_token_count, completion_token_count, 0))))
      | eval total_tokens=tonumber(trim(toString(coalesce(total_token_count, prompt_tokens+completion_tokens))))
      | eval lane="aws_bedrock_invoke_model"
      | fields _time tenant_id project model prompt_tokens completion_tokens total_tokens lane ]
    [ search index=ai_gateway sourcetype=litellm:proxy earliest=-24h@h latest=now
      | eval tenant_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(team_id, user_api_key_alias, tenant_id, "unknown_tenant"))))
      | eval project=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(metadata_project, project, "litellm_default_project"))))
      | eval model=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(model, requested_model, "unknown_model"))))
      | eval prompt_tokens=tonumber(trim(toString(coalesce(prompt_tokens, proxy_prompt_tokens, 0))))
      | eval completion_tokens=tonumber(trim(toString(coalesce(completion_tokens, proxy_completion_tokens, 0))))
      | eval total_tokens=tonumber(trim(toString(coalesce(total_tokens, prompt_tokens+completion_tokens))))
      | eval lane="litellm_ai_gateway_proxy"
      | fields _time tenant_id project model prompt_tokens completion_tokens total_tokens lane ]
    [ search index=ai_otel sourcetype=otel:gen_ai:metrics earliest=-24h@h latest=now
      | eval tenant_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(tenant_id, organization_id, "unknown_tenant"))))
      | eval project=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(gen_ai_application, project, "otel_genai_project"))))
      | eval model=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(gen_ai_response_model, model, "unknown_model"))))
      | eval prompt_tokens=tonumber(trim(toString(coalesce(gen_ai_usage_input_tokens, 0))))
      | eval completion_tokens=tonumber(trim(toString(coalesce(gen_ai_usage_output_tokens, 0))))
      | eval total_tokens=tonumber(trim(toString(coalesce(gen_ai_usage_total_tokens, prompt_tokens+completion_tokens))))
      | eval lane="otel_gen_ai_semconv_token_metrics"
      | fields _time tenant_id project model prompt_tokens completion_tokens total_tokens lane ]
| eval tenant_id=coalesce(tenant_id,"unknown_tenant")
| eval model=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(model,"unknown_model"))))
| eval model_key=model
| join type=left max=0 model_key
    [| inputlookup llm_model_pricing.csv
     | eval model_key=lower(trim(toString(model)))
     | fields model_key in_per_1k_usd out_per_1k_usd tier region]
| eval in_per_1k_usd=tonumber(coalesce(in_per_1k_usd,0.002))
| eval out_per_1k_usd=tonumber(coalesce(out_per_1k_usd,0.006))
| eval est_line_usd=round((prompt_tokens/1000)*in_per_1k_usd + (completion_tokens/1000)*out_per_1k_usd,6)
| eval tokens_per_request=total_tokens
| eval completion_to_prompt_ratio=if(prompt_tokens>0, round(completion_tokens/prompt_tokens,4), null())
| eval model_class_drift=case(match(model_key,"mini|3.5|haiku|flash"),"economy_class", match(model_key,"turbo|4o-mini|sonnet|pro"),"balanced_class", match(model_key,"4o$|^gpt-4[^-]|opus|o1|thinking"),"premium_class", true(),"unclassified_class")
| bucket _time span=5m
| stats sum(prompt_tokens) AS prompt_tokens sum(completion_tokens) AS completion_tokens sum(total_tokens) AS total_tokens sum(est_line_usd) AS est_cost_usd max(tokens_per_request) AS tokens_per_request max(completion_to_prompt_ratio) AS completion_to_prompt_ratio values(lane) AS lanes first(model_class_drift) AS model_class_drift BY _time tenant_id project model_key
| eval model=model_key
| streamstats window=12 global=f current=t sum(est_cost_usd) AS est_cost_usd_1h sum(total_tokens) AS rolling_tokens_1h BY tenant_id project model
| streamstats window=288 global=f current=t sum(est_cost_usd) AS cost_24h sum(total_tokens) AS rolling_tokens_24h BY tenant_id project model
| eventstats median(total_tokens) AS fleet_token_p50 BY tenant_id
| eventstats avg(est_cost_usd) AS env_avg_cost_bucket
| join type=left max=0 tenant_id
    [| search index=cmdb sourcetype=ai_tenant_inventory earliest=-7d@h latest=now
     | dedup tenant_id
     | eval tenant_id=lower(trim(toString(tenant_id)))
     | fields tenant_id owner_team business_unit monthly_budget_usd daily_token_quota]
| eval monthly_budget_usd=tonumber(coalesce(monthly_budget_usd,250000))
| eval daily_token_quota=tonumber(coalesce(daily_token_quota,500000000))
| eval dq_pressure=if(daily_token_quota>0, round(100*rolling_tokens_1h/(daily_token_quota/24.0),4), 0)
| streamstats window=6 global=f current=t avg(dq_pressure) AS dq_pressure_30m BY tenant_id project model
| eval monthly_budget_burn_pct=if(monthly_budget_usd>0, round(100*cost_24h/monthly_budget_usd,4), 0)
| eval daily_quota_burn_pct=if(daily_token_quota>0, round(100*rolling_tokens_24h/daily_token_quota,4), 0)
| eval severity=case(monthly_budget_burn_pct>90 OR dq_pressure_30m>80, "critical", monthly_budget_burn_pct>85 OR dq_pressure_30m>65 OR daily_quota_burn_pct>85, "high", monthly_budget_burn_pct>70 OR daily_quota_burn_pct>70 OR total_tokens>3*coalesce(fleet_token_p50,1), "medium", total_tokens>1.5*coalesce(fleet_token_p50,1) OR est_cost_usd>2*coalesce(env_avg_cost_bucket,0.000001), "low", true(), "info")
| eval recommended_action=case(severity=="critical" AND monthly_budget_burn_pct>90, "halt_discretionary_rollouts_renegotiate_budget_or_shift_traffic_to_economy_models", severity=="critical" AND dq_pressure_30m>80, "enforce_quota_throttles_partition_keys_and_request_capacity_uplift", severity=="high", "trace_model_router_changes_revert_unapproved_upgrades_validate_rag_context_limits", severity=="medium", "tighten_gateway_metadata_tags_review_prompt_templates_and_batch_policies", severity=="low", "sample_requests_compare_tokenizers_and_multimodal_attachment_rules", true(), "continue_finops_reconciliation_refresh_price_lookup_and_cmdb_budgets")
| table _time tenant_id project owner_team business_unit model total_tokens prompt_tokens completion_tokens est_cost_usd_1h monthly_budget_usd monthly_budget_burn_pct daily_quota_burn_pct severity recommended_action
cimSpl:
| tstats summariesonly=true latest(Performance.cpu_pct) AS cpu_pct FROM datamodel=Performance WHERE nodename=Performance earliest=-24h@h latest=now BY Performance.host
| rename Performance.host AS metric_host
| join type=left max=0 metric_host [| tstats summariesonly=true latest(Inventory.cpu_arch) AS cpu_arch FROM datamodel=Inventory WHERE nodename=Inventory earliest=-24h@h latest=now BY Inventory.dest | rename Inventory.dest AS metric_host]
| where like(lower(metric_host),"infer%") OR like(lower(metric_host),"gpu%") OR like(lower(metric_host),"llm%")
| table metric_host cpu_pct cpu_arch
dataModelAcceleration: Accelerate Performance summaries for gateway and inference hosts modeled as Performance.host when correlating token export pipeline pressure; accelerate Inventory summaries for Inventory.dest when mapping hardware classes that host self-managed vLLM or Ollama endpoints; retain thirty days for monthly FinOps attestations.
visualization: Severity-colored table by tenant_id, project, and model with drilldowns to raw multisearch lanes, lookup-enriched price tier, and cmdb owner_team; dual-axis chart of est_cost_usd_1h versus rolling_tokens_24h; single-value tiles for monthly_budget_burn_pct and daily_quota_burn_pct.

references.title: Microsoft Learn — Plan and manage costs for Azure OpenAI Service
OpenTelemetry — Generative AI metrics semantic conventions

knownFalsePositives: Scheduled batch backfill jobs that replay historical chats through a gateway can spike token totals for a day without implying live customer abuse; require change_ticket_id correlation before paging product owners. Fine-tuning evaluation passes that sweep many prompts against a teacher model inflate completion tokens predictably; join registry flags that mark eval suites as non-production spend. Planned migration onto a larger model class with written budget approval will raise est_cost_usd_1h while dq_pressure stays controlled; verify CAB records and FinOps approval artifacts. Model-deprecation re-encoding campaigns that rewrite corpora into new tokenizer families can multiply prompt tokens briefly; treat as benign when catalog notes a deprecation window. Embeddings re-index after a documentation refresh can dominate token volume while chat traffic is quiet; separate embedding routes in gateway metadata to avoid misrouting on-call. Reserved capacity or provisioned throughput purchases can change effective marginal price without changing token counters; refresh llm_model_pricing.csv when contracts renew. Multimodal attachments counted under provider-specific image or audio token rules may look like prompt bloat until you align tokenizer documentation with the emitted usage fields. Synthetic canary traffic that intentionally exercises premium models for latency checks will move cost needles; tag canary tenants and downgrade non-production severities. Azure OpenAI diagnostic lag during Event Hub throttling can delay billing arms minutes behind live consoles; confirm pipeline health before declaring false negatives. Duplicate HEC submissions from blue-green forwarders during cutovers can double-count tokens until dedupe keys land; compare with vendor usage dashboards.
hardware: Applies to cloud-managed LLM endpoints, Kubernetes-hosted OpenTelemetry Collectors and AI gateway proxies, AWS Bedrock invoke log pipelines, Azure OpenAI billing exports, and self-hosted vLLM or Ollama hosts that emit token usage into forwarded logs.
exclusions: Does not replace UC-13.4.1 latency and HTTP error analytics, UC-13.4.3 GPU and TPU utilization economics, dedicated prompt injection classifiers, or model quality evaluation suites. Does not change provider quotas, budgets, or gateway routes; it produces evidence and recommended actions for human-approved changes.

controlTest.positiveScenario: In a lab tenant, route synthetic traffic through a premium model while daily_token_quota and monthly_budget_usd are set low in ai_tenant_inventory, forward all five multisearch lanes, confirm platform_uc_13_4_2_llm_token_cost_quota_burn returns critical or high with non-null recommended_action within two five-minute windows.
controlTest.negativeScenario: After restoring approved model routes and retrieval limits, when rolling token sums return near baseline and cmdb budgets align with vendor consoles, confirm severities return to info or low and the alert wrapper emits zero rows when filtering to critical and high only.

evidence: Saved search platform_uc_13_4_2_llm_token_cost_quota_burn on a five-minute schedule; dashboard drilldowns across proxy, billing, Bedrock, gateway, and OTel sourcetypes; alert routing to platform FinOps and ML gateway on-call; weekly CSV exports archived to a restricted evidence index.
